reason: Significant regulatory headwinds from increased alleged crypto theft and an alarming 700% surge in sanctions evasion using crypto pose substantial systemic risks and could trigger stringent government action. Adding to this, a major miner (CleanSpark) offloading 97% of its Bitcoin production to fund an AI pivot creates direct selling pressure and indicates a potential shift in capital allocation away from Bitcoin. Bitcoin's current pullback despite a soaring software sector suggests internal weakness and a lack of correlation with broader tech strength. These factors point towards a highly cautious immediate outlook with potential for further downside.
headlines: Son of U.S. government contractor, accused of stealing millions in seized crypto, arrested in France
Sanctions evasions using crypto increased by 700% in 2025: Chainalysis
CleanSpark sold 97% of February bitcoin production to fund AI pivot
Crypto for Advisors: Tokenization offers always-on global investment access
Bitcoin pulls back to near $71,000 even as software sector soars
